meaning: adj.不能复归的,不能削减的,[数]不可约分的
examples: The stink of poop is not an irreducible fact about the universe.
Let me first address this concept of irreducible complexity.
Maybe, after all, poetry is an expression of the irreducible conflict of our needs.
That irreducible tension led Huffington to pursue a sale and push her partners to go along.
After all, housing and capital spending have wound down to near irreducible desuetude.
